module.name: equation
module.description: A centered display equation with a right-aligned equation number, e.g. (1). Body = the LaTeX (no delimiters needed).
module.aiSpec:
A numbered DISPLAY EQUATION — the formula shown large and centered with an equation number off to the side, like in a paper. Block module.
Body: just the LaTeX of the equation. Do NOT wrap it in \[ \] or $$ — the module adds the display delimiters itself. One equation per module; use several modules with different `number`s to reference them from your prose.
When NOT to use: for a titled formula with a "where" legend use @formula; for inline math in a sentence just write \( ... \) directly.
Parameters: - `number` : the tag shown in parentheses on the side, e.g. 1 or 3.2. Default blank (no number). Any short text. - `align` : left | center | right — horizontal alignment of the formula. Default center. - `numberside` : left | right — which side the (number) sits on. Default right. - `size` : small | normal | large — overall size of the equation. Default normal. - `color` : color of the (number) tag. Default the muted theme color. Any CSS color.
<!-- @equation number: 1, size: large --> E = mc^2 <!-- @end -->

param.name: number
param.label: Equation number
param.desc: Tag shown in parentheses, e.g. 1 or 3.2; blank = no number.

param.name: numberside
param.label: Number side
param.desc: Which side the (number) tag sits on.

param.name: size
param.label: Size
param.desc: Overall size of the displayed equation.

snippet.label: Numbered equation
snippet.description: Centered display equation with a right-aligned (n) tag. Body is LaTeX (no delimiters).
